channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Haitian migrants in Springfield, Ohio, turn to faith amid deportation fears
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ohio (AP) — At the end of his Sunday service, the pastor of the First Haitian Evangelical Church of Springfield, Ohio, asked ushers and musicians to form a circle around him as he knelt in prayer, flanked by the flags of Haiti and the United States.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO and JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Top Trump administration officials, including “border czar” Tom Homan and the acting deputy attorney general, visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcementin the nation’s third-largest city as federal agencies touted arrests around the country.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Colombian president orders increase of import tariffs on US goods after Trump order
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro, in a post on the social platform X, said he ordered the "foreign trade minister to raise import tariffs from the U.S. by 25%.
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: Ryan Pressly waives no-trade clause to join the Chicago Cubs, AP source says
item.description: The Chicago Cubs are finalizing a trade for Ryan Pressly, adding the right-hander to the back end of their bullpen in their second major offseason deal with the Houston Astros.
item.dc:creator: JAY COHEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: Court records
item.description: Matthew and Sarah McGrath filed a lawsuit in February 2023, claiming a house built for them a year earlier had numerous defects,
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, NewsNation

item.title: A Sundance documentary called ‘The Stringer’ disputes who took AP’s 'napalm girl' photo in Vietnam
item.description: The authorship of The Associated Press’s renowned “napalm girl” photograph is being called into question in the new documentary “The Stringer,” which premiered at the Sundance Film Festival in Utah.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: ICE detains nearly 50 people in 'makeshift nightclub' raid in Denver area: DEA
item.description: "For the last several months, DEA has been running an ongoing investigation against the Venezuelan gang called TdA," special agent Jonathan Pullen said.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: court records
item.description: A settlement has been reached in the lawsuit involving an Arkansas-based couple and stars of HGTV's "Fixer to Fabulous" just days before the parties were set to go to trial.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KRON4

item.title: Belarus frees American woman from detention as strongman leader looks to extend 30-year rule
item.description: Belarus has “unilaterally” freed an American woman from detention, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced Sunday, as the Kremlin-allied country held an orchestrated election poised to give strongman President Alexander Lukashenko yet another term on top of his three decades in power.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Husband of nurse, mom of three found dead at bottom of staircase went for beer after finding body: police
item.description: Authorities are investigating the death of a Utah nurse, whose husband allegedly told police he went out to get beer after finding her body at the bottom of a stairwell.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Freighter on the move after it was freed from ice on frozen Lake Erie
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — A freighter was on its way to Canada on Sunday after it was freed from ice that trapped it in a frozen Lake Erie for days, the U.S. Coast Guard said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More DEI fallout: Air Force scraps course that used videos of Tuskegee Airmen and female WWII pilots
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Air Force has removed training courses with videos of its storied Tuskegee Airmen and the Women Airforce Service Pilots, or WASPs — the female World War II pilots who were vital in ferrying warplanes for the military — to comply with the Trump administration's crackdown on dive
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As LA schools prepare to reopen, toxic ash raises concerns
item.description: As parents in Los Angeles County prepare to send their children back to the schools in wildfire impacted areas that are still standing, parents are wondering how safe they are.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli firefighter battling LA fires says California has similarity to home that could give her advantage
item.description: A delegation of Israeli firefighting experts spent a week in California helping American crews combat the LA fires. One member of the team described the experience as an "honor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Black History Month movie celebration at AMC Theatres
item.description: The movie theater chain will bring back seven fan-favorite films from the past year for the month of February. Tickets for each show are already available to purchase online.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, WGN 9

item.title: Freighter remains stuck in the ice on a frozen Lake Erie
item.description: The U.S. and Canadian Coast Guards on Saturday continued efforts to break up ice and free a freighter that has been trapped in a frozen Lake Erie for days, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A Washington state woman has been charged in the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont, which happened days after authorities began watching her and a German companion who also died in the roadside shootout, the FBI said Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hamas frees 4 female Israeli soldiers in exchange for 200 Palestinian prisoners as ceasefire holds
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Four female Israeli soldiers who were taken in the attack that sparked the war in Gaza returned to Israel on Saturday after Hamas militants paraded them before a crowd of thousands in Gaza City and handed them over to the Red Cross.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, SAM MEDNICK, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Ozempic and similar weight loss drugs may lower risk of 42 health conditions, but also pose risks
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration’s approval of Ozempic in 2021 for weight loss treatment ushered in a new era for the class of drugs called glucagon-like peptide-1 agonists, or GLP-1.
item.dc:creator: Ziyad Al-Aly Clinical Epidemiologist, Washington University in St. Louis, KTLA 5

item.title: Ohio school bus driver rescues family's pets from burning home
item.description: Bitter cold temperatures closed schools in a Stark County community on Wednesday, so it was a day off for Todd Morris as well.
item.dc:creator: Dave Nethers, Fox 5

item.title: Tattoo removal expert says 1 in 7 clients asks to remove this specific tattoo
item.description: Today’s tattoo-removal experts are quite adept at ridding their clients of all types of ill-conceived tattoos. But there are a few specific categories of ill-conceived ink that they tend to treat more often than others.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: As schools in LA reopen, parents worry about harmful ash from wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Kelli Ferrone wiped blinds and window sills inside her daughters’ elementary school, just a half mile from the devastating Palisades Fire that scorched the coastal enclave in Los Angeles. The cotton swabs and wipes came up black. There is scattered ash and soot at her feet.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The only person in the world with a functioning pig organ is thriving after a record 2 months
item.description: An Alabama woman passed a major milestone Saturday to become the longest living recipient of a pig organ transplant – healthy and full of energy with her new kidney for 61 days and counting.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Australian Open: Keys upsets 2-time champion Sabalenka in women's final for 1st Grand Slam title
item.description: When Madison Keys stepped into Rod Laver Arena at 7:37 p.m. on Saturday night ahead of the Australian Open final, she strode right past the Daphne Akhurst Memorial Cup, the trophy that goes to the women's champion and was placed on a pedestal near the entrance to the court.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Aurora borealis returns: When and where to watch the northern lights
item.description: Sky gazers in several U.S. states could get a colorful glimpse of the northern lights as we enter the weekend, thanks to a recent geomagnetic storm.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Trump cuts Dr. Fauci's security detail
item.description: Asked if he would feel partially responsible if something were to happen to Fauci, Trump said he would not.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: In policy reversal, Trump eliminates help for Black and Latino communities hit harder by pollution
item.description: President Trump is reversing his predecessor’s efforts to clean up areas hardest hit by pollution from heavy industry, ports and roadways – communities that are often largely Black, Latino and low-income.
item.dc:creator: Michael Phillis And Alexa St. John, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Secret Service says it visited Chicago elementary school, not ICE
item.description: Chicago Public Schools officials said in a press conference Friday that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents showed up at an elementary school in the city but were denied entry. But later on Friday, the Secret Service said it visited the school, not ICE.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Spirit Airlines updates dress code, tattoo policy for passengers
item.description: Passengers flying on Spirit Airlines can now be removed from a flight if a tattoo or other "body art" is deemed "lewd, obscene, or offensive.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, Fox 5

item.title: Marilyn Manson won't face domestic violence and sexual assault charges, DA says
item.description: "We recognize and applaud the courage and resilience of the women who came forward to make reports and share their experiences, and we thank them for their cooperation and patience with the investigation," the district attorney's office said in a news release explaining the decision not to pursue ch
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Trump proposes 'getting rid of FEMA' during North Carolina visit
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was considering "getting rid of FEMA” as he visited hurricane-battered North Carolina on Friday during the first trip of his second term.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and CHRIS MEGERIAN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Progress is made on a huge fire north of Los Angeles while new fires erupt in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Evacuation orders were lifted Thursday for tens of thousands as firefighters with air support slowed the spread of a huge wildfire churning through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but new blazes erupted in San Diego County, briefly triggering more evacuations.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4
